title: Fallout From Iran War and Oil Shock Deliver Another Blow to World Economy
summary: Countries already walloped by a breakdown of the international trading order, war in Ukraine and chaotic U.S. policymaking are facing potentially lasting economic damage.

title: How Hegseth Came to See Moral Purpose in War as Weakness
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s bellicose and vengeful rhetoric describing the military’s war in Iran grew out of his experience in Iraq.

title: Why Does Oil Matter So Much to the Global Economy?
summary: The war in the Middle East has sent oil and gas prices soaring. Our energy reporter Rebecca F. Elliott breaks down how much the world continues to depend on reliable sources of oil.

title: Judge Dismisses Lawsuit Over Trump’s Funding Freeze for Gateway Tunnel
summary: The case is moot, a federal judge said, because the administration has resumed the flow of more than $200 million in funding that it had temporarily stopped.

title: A Call for Reporting Tips Rankles Pentagon Officials
summary: A Washington Post appeal for information about the military qualified as prohibited “solicitation,” according to defense officials.

title: James Clyburn to Run for House Again, Defying Push for Generational Change
summary: The Democratic power broker had kept his plans under wraps but signaled he wanted to be around to see the first Black speaker elected, a milestone the party is well positioned to reach next year.

summary: The president called Mr. Paul, a boxer and provocative influencer, a “great guy” and predicted that he would run for an elected position.

title: Bloomberg to Back Protégé in Crowded N.Y.C. House Race With Super PAC
summary: The billionaire is said to be willing to spend up to $5 million to help Assemblyman Micah Lasher in his bid to replace Representative Jerrold Nadler, bypassing a Kennedy scion, Jack Schlossberg.

title: Videos of ICE Shooting in Texas Capture a Confused and Fatal Encounter
summary: Officials said a 23-year-old Texan had intentionally run over an officer, a claim his family and friend denied. Newly released footage leaves the truth murky.

summary: “Sinners” and “One Battle After Another” have been neck and neck all season. Here’s what our expert thinks will go the distance.

summary: Ahead of the Oscars on Sunday, Food and Film writers and editors recount the moments that filled them most.

summary: The globally acclaimed restaurant had the power and influence to remake restaurant culture. Instead, it perpetuated the darkest parts of that world.

summary: In the era of A.I. agents, many Silicon Valley programmers are now barely programming. Instead, what they’re doing is deeply, deeply weird.

summary: Residents of Southaven, Miss., have complained about noise and cited concerns about possible pollution from gas turbines that will help xAI train its chatbot.

summary: President Trump is tempering the political ambitions of María Corina Machado, a Nobel laureate, as he deepens ties with her foes in Venezuela.

title: With New Right-Wing President, Chile Shifts Toward Region’s Conservative, Pro-Trump Alignment
summary: The inauguration of Chile’s new president, José Antonio Kast, is the latest milestone in a broader shift toward conservatism and pro-Trump alignment in the region.

summary: A New Jersey jury found Harris Jacobs guilty of leaving the scene of an accident after he struck a pedestrian with his S.U.V. But jurors would not have the final word.

summary: Closing arguments began Thursday in a landmark trial that put online behavior under a microscope. In many ways, it has already been decided.

summary: Visitors are flocking to see a bonanza of wildflowers that has transformed this barren desert.

title: March Heat Wave Could Topple Records in Los Angeles and Phoenix
summary: Downtown Los Angeles could get into the 90s, and Phoenix was on track to hit 100.

summary: The climate pattern known for intense heat, floods and drought is likely to develop this summer, raising questions about disaster preparedness.

summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani attended the third “rental rip-off” hearing in New York City, sitting down with three tenants to hear their woes and assuaging protesters.

title: Pardoning Netanyahu Now Would Be Improper, Key Israeli Office Says
